_comment: Plugin-bundled PreToolUse hooks auto-approve the tools that the pipeline calls many times per run (specifically across subagent dispatch boundaries, where Cowork's 'Always allow' UI does NOT persist per #24433). Four independent matcher blocks: (1) cowork artifact tools that power the live-progress dashboard (v0.5.4+); (2) visualize widget tools that power Phase 1.5 mode mockup, Phase 2a/6.6 elicitation, Phase 3 plan diagram, Phase 12 final dashboard, and the 5 milestone trackers (v0.6.3+); (3) WebSearch — unconditional auto-allow because WebSearch surfaces no specific URL and is used heavily by currency-checker + doctrinal-researcher across every memo (v0.8.0+); (4) WebFetch — auto-allow restricted to a curated legal-research domain allowlist (regulators / national DPAs / courts / standards bodies / privacy NGOs) via inline urlparse host suffix-match, so doctrinal-researcher's WebFetch fallback to EDPB / Commission / DPA URLs (when LDH doctrine namespace is thin) and currency-checker's status pulls no longer prompt (v0.8.0+). Block 4's matcher also covers Cowork's MCP-provided fetch tool (`^mcp__.*fetch$`, e.g. `mcp__workspace__web_fetch`) and its inline Python extracts the URL across input-key shapes (`url`/`uri`/`link`/`href`/… then a regex scan of any string value): in some Cowork environments web fetching is exposed as an MCP tool rather than the native `WebFetch`, so a `^WebFetch$`-only matcher silently never fired and EVERY research fetch — even allowlisted EDPB/ICO — prompted (empirically observed in the 2026-05-29 run where fetches logged as `mcp__workspace__web_fetch` while searches logged as native `WebSearch`; fixed v1.1.1, which also added `artificialintelligenceact.eu`). The host allowlist gate is unchanged, so the broader matcher only widens what gets CHECKED — non-allowlisted hosts still prompt. All four use inline `python3 -c` invocation — Cowork's hook executor does NOT expand `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}` on Windows (empirically falsified 2026-05-26, see CHANGELOG v0.5.7), so script-file approaches fail; inline form depends only on `python3` being on PATH. The matcher regexes restrict the hook to specific tools so the inline Python can unconditionally output `allow` (blocks 1–3) or do an O(1) host-suffix check (block 4). The visualize matcher requires `visualize` substring inside the namespace token so an arbitrary user MCP exposing `show_widget` won't be auto-approved unless its namespace declares itself a visualize variant (covers both plugin-scoped `mcp__plugin_visualize_*__show_widget` and Cowork UUID-scoped `mcp__<uuid-with-visualize-tag>__show_widget`). The WebFetch allowlist includes (a) `europa.eu` covering all `*.europa.eu` subdomains via suffix match (eur-lex, ec, edpb, edps, consilium, europarl, curia, enisa, esma, eba, eiopa, ecb, ema, echa, digital-strategy), (b) `coe.int` (Council of Europe + ECtHR), (c) 37 EU/EEA/UK/CH national DPAs, (d) ~24 US federal portals + state privacy regulators + open case-law databases, (e) ~14 international/standards bodies (WIPO, OECD, UN, OHCHR, WTO, BIS, FATF, ISO, IEC, ETSI, CEN, CENELEC, IETF, W3C), (f) 4 privacy NGO / community trackers (noyb, GDPRhub, IAPP, EDRi). Private law-firm blogs and ad-hoc commentary publishers are NOT included — users grant those via README fallback (~/.claude/settings.json) if needed. LDH/CourtListener tools are NOT auto-approved here — those surface under arbitrary UUID namespaces in Cowork and have generic function names (`get_document`/`search`) that could collide with other MCPs; users grant them via README fallback. Deny / ask rules in user settings still take precedence over this hook's allow decision. See: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks and https://code.claude.com/docs/en/permissions for hook spec.

hooks.command: python3 -c "import json,sys; sys.stdout.write(json.dumps({'hookSpecificOutput':{'hookEventName':'PreToolUse','permissionDecision':'allow','permissionDecisionReason':'memoforge visualize widgets pre-approved (Phase 1.5 mockup / Phase 2a + 6.6 elicitation / Phase 3 plan diagram / Phase 12 final dashboard / milestone trackers)'}}))"

python3 -c "import json,sys; sys.stdout.write(json.dumps({'hookSpecificOutput':{'hookEventName':'PreToolUse','permissionDecision':'allow','permissionDecisionReason':'memoforge research WebSearch pre-approved (currency-checker + doctrinal-researcher heavy users)'}}))"

python3 -c "import json,sys,re; from urllib.parse import urlparse as p; A=frozenset('ada.gov aepd.es aki.ee artificialintelligenceact.eu autoriteprotectiondonnees.be autoriteitpersoonsgegevens.nl azop.hr baylda.de bfdi.bund.de bis.org cen.eu cenelec.eu cisa.gov cnil.fr cnpd.public.lu cnpd.pt coe.int congress.gov courtlistener.com cppa.ca.gov cpdp.bg datatilsynet.dk datatilsynet.no datenschutz-berlin.de datenschutz.hessen.de datenschutzkonferenz-online.de dataprotection.gov.cy dataprotection.gov.sk dataprotection.ro dol.gov dpa.gr dpc.ie dvi.gov.lv ecfr.gov edoeb.admin.ch edri.org eeoc.gov etsi.org europa.eu fatf-gafi.org federalregister.gov findlaw.com ftc.gov garanteprivacy.it gdprhub.eu govinfo.gov hhs.gov iapp.org ico.org.uk idpc.org.mt iec.ch ietf.org imy.se ip-rs.si irs.gov iso.org justia.com justice.gov law.cornell.edu lda.bayern.de ldi.nrw.de naih.hu nist.gov nlrb.gov noyb.eu oag.ca.gov oecd.org ohchr.org personuvernd.is sec.gov supremecourt.gov tietosuoja.fi un.org uodo.gov.pl uoou.cz uscourts.gov vdai.lrv.lt w3.org whitehouse.gov wipo.int wto.org'.split()); d=json.loads(sys.stdin.read()); ti=(d.get('tool_input') or {}); u=next((mm.group(0) for s in [ti.get(k) for k in ('url','uri','link','href','address','target')]+list(ti.values()) if isinstance(s,str) for mm in [re.search('https?://[^ ]+',s)] if mm),''); h=(p(u).hostname or '').lower(); ok=bool(h) and (h in A or any(h.endswith('.'+x) for x in A)); sys.stdout.write(json.dumps({'hookSpecificOutput':{'hookEventName':'PreToolUse','permissionDecision':'allow','permissionDecisionReason':'memoforge legal-research domain allowlist hit: '+h}}) if ok else '{}')"
